title: Add Promise.race() timeout protection on all bot.pathfinder.goto() calls
description:
Add hard timeout protection on every `await bot.pathfinder.goto(...)` call to prevent silent hangs. The known mineflayer-pathfinder issue (#222, #273) causes goto() to never resolve when the bot is obstructed by unbreakable blocks or certain edge cases.

Currently 7 goto() calls exist with NO timeout (confirmed by grep). Each needs a Promise.race() wrapper.

Implementation:
1. Create a `gotoWithTimeout(goal, timeoutMs)` helper function:
   ```js
   async function gotoWithTimeout(goal, timeoutMs) {
     const gotoPromise = bot.pathfinder.goto(goal);
     const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
       setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(`Pathfinder timeout after ${timeoutMs}ms`)), timeoutMs)
     );
     return Promise.race([gotoPromise, timeoutPromise]);
   }
   ```
2. When the timeout wins:
   - Call `bot.pathfinder.setGoal(null)` to cancel the active path.
   - Log warning with context (action, target coords, correlationId).
   - Emit `pathUpdate` event with status `timeout` and correlationId.
   - Return failure to the action dispatcher so the C# side gets a clean failure.
3. Tunable constants per action type:
   - `MOVE_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000` (move case)
   - `MINE_GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000` (goto to ore block)
   - `PLACE_GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000` (goto to placement site)
   - `CRAFT_GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000` (goto to crafting table)
   - `WANDER_GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000` (wander)
   - `SMELT_GOTO_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000` (goto to furnace)
4. Replace ALL 7 bare `await bot.pathfinder.goto(...)` calls with `await gotoWithTimeout(...)`.

Acceptance:
- No goto() call runs longer than its timeout.
- Timeout emits a clean failure event back to C# (not a silent hang).
- Active pathfinder is cancelled on timeout.
- All 7 call sites updated.

Refs: `mineflayer-adapter-research.md` DEF-PAPER-3; `msa_planning_mineflayer_audit_20260621_1437.md` Finding 6

comments.author: SteveBot
comments.body: Code audit 2026-06-27: Confirmed 7 unprotected goto() calls across move/mine/place/wander/craft/smelt. Precedent exists: findReachableBlock already uses getPathTo({ timeout }) — same pattern should work for goto() calls. The gotoWithTimeout helper must cancel active pathfinder (bot.pathfinder.setGoal(null)) on timeout and emit a clean error event so C# gets failure feedback (not a silent hang). See /memories/repo/adapter-goto-timeout-gap.md for the full call-site map.
